1	import torch
2	
3	from diffusers import DDPMScheduler
4	
5	from .test_schedulers import SchedulerCommonTest
6	
7	
8	class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
9	    scheduler_classes = (DDPMScheduler,)
10	
11	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
12	        config = {
13	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
14	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
15	            "beta_end": 0.02,
16	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
17	            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
18	            "clip_sample": True,
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def test_timesteps(self):
25	        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
26	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
27	
28	    def test_betas(self):
29	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
30	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
31	
32	    def test_schedules(self):
33	        for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
34	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
35	
36	    def test_variance_type(self):
37	        for variance in ["fixed_small", "fixed_large", "other"]:
38	            self.check_over_configs(variance_type=variance)
39	
40	    def test_clip_sample(self):
41	        for clip_sample in [True, False]:
42	            self.check_over_configs(clip_sample=clip_sample)
43	
44	    def test_thresholding(self):
45	        self.check_over_configs(thresholding=False)
46	        for threshold in [0.5, 1.0, 2.0]:
47	            for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
48	                self.check_over_configs(
49	                    thresholding=True,
50	                    prediction_type=prediction_type,
51	                    sample_max_value=threshold,
52	                )
53	
54	    def test_prediction_type(self):
55	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
56	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
57	
58	    def test_time_indices(self):
59	        for t in [0, 500, 999]:
60	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t)
61	
62	    def test_variance(self):
63	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
64	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
65	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
66	
67	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
68	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(487) - 0.00979)) < 1e-5
69	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999) - 0.02)) < 1e-5
70	
71	    def test_rescale_betas_zero_snr(self):
72	        for rescale_betas_zero_snr in [True, False]:
73	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
74	
75	    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
76	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
77	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
78	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
79	
80	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
81	
82	        model = self.dummy_model()
83	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
84	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
85	
86	        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
87	            # 1. predict noise residual
88	            residual = model(sample, t)
89	
90	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1
91	            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
92	
93	            # if t > 0:
94	            #     noise = self.dummy_sample_deter
95	            #     variance = scheduler.get_variance(t) ** (0.5) * noise
96	            #
97	            # sample = pred_prev_sample + variance
98	            sample = pred_prev_sample
99	
100	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
101	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
102	
103	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
104	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.3372) < 1e-3
105	
106	    def test_full_loop_with_v_prediction(self):
107	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
108	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(prediction_type="v_prediction")
109	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
110	
111	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
112	
113	        model = self.dummy_model()
114	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
115	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
116	
117	        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
118	            # 1. predict noise residual
119	            residual = model(sample, t)
120	
121	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1
122	            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
123	
124	            # if t > 0:
125	            #     noise = self.dummy_sample_deter
126	            #     variance = scheduler.get_variance(t) ** (0.5) * noise
127	            #
128	            # sample = pred_prev_sample + variance
129	            sample = pred_prev_sample
130	
131	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
132	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
133	
134	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 202.0296) < 1e-2
135	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.2631) < 1e-3
136	
137	    def test_custom_timesteps(self):
138	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
139	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
140	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
141	
142	        timesteps = [100, 87, 50, 1, 0]
143	
144	        scheduler.set_timesteps(timesteps=timesteps)
145	
146	        scheduler_timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
147	
148	        for i, timestep in enumerate(scheduler_timesteps):
149	            if i == len(timesteps) - 1:
150	                expected_prev_t = -1
151	            else:
152	                expected_prev_t = timesteps[i + 1]
153	
154	            prev_t = scheduler.previous_timestep(timestep)
155	            prev_t = prev_t.item()
156	
157	            self.assertEqual(prev_t, expected_prev_t)
158	
159	    def test_custom_timesteps_increasing_order(self):
160	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
161	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
162	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
163	
164	        timesteps = [100, 87, 50, 51, 0]
165	
166	        with self.assertRaises(ValueError, msg="`custom_timesteps` must be in descending order."):
167	            scheduler.set_timesteps(timesteps=timesteps)
168	
169	    def test_custom_timesteps_passing_both_num_inference_steps_and_timesteps(self):
170	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
171	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
172	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
173	
174	        timesteps = [100, 87, 50, 1, 0]
175	        num_inference_steps = len(timesteps)
176	
177	        with self.assertRaises(ValueError, msg="Can only pass one of `num_inference_steps` or `custom_timesteps`."):
178	            scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps, timesteps=timesteps)
179	
180	    def test_custom_timesteps_too_large(self):
181	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
182	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
183	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
184	
185	        timesteps = [scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps]
186	
187	        with self.assertRaises(
188	            ValueError,
189	            msg="`timesteps` must start before `self.config.train_timesteps`: {scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps}}",
190	        ):
191	            scheduler.set_timesteps(timesteps=timesteps)
192	
193	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
194	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
195	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
196	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
197	
198	        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
199	        t_start = num_trained_timesteps - 2
200	
201	        model = self.dummy_model()
202	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
203	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
204	
205	        # add noise
206	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
207	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
208	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
209

211	            # 1. predict noise residual
212	            residual = model(sample, t)
213	
214	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1

217	
218	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
219	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
220	
221	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 387.9466) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 387.9466, but get {result_sum}"
222	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.5051) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 0.5051, but get {result_mean}"

314	    @property
315	    def dummy_sample(self):
316	        batch_size = 4
317	        num_channels = 3
318	        height = 8
319	        width = 8
320	
321	        sample = torch.rand((batch_size, num_channels, height, width))
322	
323	        return sample
324	
325	    @property
326	    def dummy_noise_deter(self):
327	        batch_size = 4
328	        num_channels = 3
329	        height = 8
330	        width = 8
331	
332	        num_elems = batch_size * num_channels * height * width
333	        sample = torch.arange(num_elems).flip(-1)
334	        sample = sample.reshape(num_channels, height, width, batch_size)
335	        sample = sample / num_elems
336	        sample = sample.permute(3, 0, 1, 2)
337	
338	        return sample
339	
340	    @property
341	    def dummy_sample_deter(self):
342	        batch_size = 4
343	        num_channels = 3
344	        height = 8
345	        width = 8
346	
347	        num_elems = batch_size * num_channels * height * width
348	        sample = torch.arange(num_elems)
349	        sample = sample.reshape(num_channels, height, width, batch_size)
350	        sample = sample / num_elems
351	        sample = sample.permute(3, 0, 1, 2)
352	
353	        return sample

355	    def get_scheduler_config(self):
356	        raise NotImplementedError
357	
358	    def dummy_model(self):
359	        def model(sample, t, *args):
360	            # if t is a tensor, match the number of dimensions of sample
361	            if isinstance(t, torch.Tensor):
362	                num_dims = len(sample.shape)
363	                # pad t with 1s to match num_dims
364	                t = t.reshape(-1, *(1,) * (num_dims - 1)).to(sample.device, dtype=sample.dtype)
365	
366	            return sample * t / (t + 1)
367	
368	        return model
369	
370	    def check_over_configs(self, time_step=0, **config):
371	        kwargs = dict(self.forward_default_kwargs)
372	
373	        num_inference_steps = kwargs.pop("num_inference_steps", None)
374	        time_step = time_step if time_step is not None else self.default_timestep
375	
376	        for scheduler_class in self.scheduler_classes:
377	            # TODO(Suraj) - delete the following two lines once DDPM, DDIM, and PNDM have timesteps casted to float by default
378	            if scheduler_class in (EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler, LMSDiscreteScheduler):
379	                time_step = float(time_step)
380	
381	            scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
382	            scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
383	
384	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
385	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
386	                scaled_sigma_max = scheduler.sigma_to_t(scheduler.config.sigma_max)
387	                time_step = scaled_sigma_max
388	
389	            if scheduler_class == EDMEulerScheduler:
390	                time_step = scheduler.timesteps[-1]
391	
392	            if scheduler_class == VQDiffusionScheduler:
393	                num_vec_classes = scheduler_config["num_vec_classes"]
394	                sample = self.dummy_sample(num_vec_classes)
395	                model = self.dummy_model(num_vec_classes)
396	                residual = model(sample, time_step)
397	            else:
398	                sample = self.dummy_sample
399	                residual = 0.1 * sample

28	class CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
29	    """
30	    Implements a variant of `DPMSolverMultistepScheduler` with cosine schedule, proposed by Nichol and Dhariwal (2021).
31	    This scheduler was used in Stable Audio Open [1].
32	
33	    [1] Evans, Parker, et al. "Stable Audio Open" https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.14358
34	
35	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
36	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
37	
38	    Args:
39	        sigma_min (`float`, defaults to `0.3`):
40	            Minimum noise magnitude in the sigma schedule. This was set to 0.3 in Stable Audio Open [1].
41	        sigma_max (`float`, defaults to `500`):
42	            Maximum noise magnitude in the sigma schedule. This was set to 500 in Stable Audio Open [1].
43	        sigma_data (`float`, defaults to `1.0`):
44	            The standard deviation of the data distribution. This is set to 1.0 in Stable Audio Open [1].
45	        sigma_schedule (`str`, defaults to `"exponential"`):
46	            Sigma schedule to compute the `sigmas`. Must be one of `"exponential"` or `"karras"`. The exponential
47	            schedule was incorporated in [stabilityai/cosxl](https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/cosxl). The Karras
48	            schedule is introduced in the [EDM](https://huggingface.co/papers/2206.00364) paper.
49	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to `1000`):
50	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
51	        solver_order (`int`, defaults to `2`):
52	            The DPMSolver order which can be `1` or `2`. It is recommended to use `solver_order=2`.
53	        prediction_type (`str`, defaults to `"v_prediction"`):
54	            Prediction type of the scheduler function. Must be one of `"epsilon"` (predicts the noise of the diffusion
55	            process), `"sample"` (directly predicts the noisy sample), or `"v_prediction"` (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
56	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper).
57	        rho (`float`, defaults to `7.0`):
58	            The parameter for calculating the Karras sigma schedule from the EDM
59	            [paper](https://huggingface.co/papers/2206.00364).
60	        solver_type (`str`, defaults to `"midpoint"`):
61	            Solver type for the second-order solver. Must be one of `"midpoint"` or `"heun"`. The solver type slightly
62	            affects the sample quality, especially for a small number of steps. It is recommended to use `"midpoint"`.
63	        lower_order_final (`bool`, defaults to `True`):
64	            Whether to use lower-order solvers in the final steps. Only valid for < 15 inference steps. This can
65	            stabilize the sampling of DPMSolver for steps < 15, especially for steps <= 10.
66	        euler_at_final (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
67	            Whether to use Euler's method in the final step. It is a trade-off between numerical stability and detail
68	            richness. This can stabilize the sampling of the SDE variant of DPMSolver for small number of inference
69	            steps, but sometimes may result in blurring.
70	        final_sigmas_type (`str`, defaults to `"zero"`):
71	            The final `sigma` value for the noise schedule during the sampling process. Must be one of `"zero"` or
72	            `"sigma_min"`. If `"sigma_min"`, the final sigma is the same as the last sigma in the training schedule. If
73	            `"zero"`, the final sigma is set to 0.
74	    """
75	
76	    _compatibles = []
77	    order = 1
78	
79	    @register_to_config
80	    def __init__(
81	        self,
82	        sigma_min: float = 0.3,
83	        sigma_max: float = 500,
84	        sigma_data: float = 1.0,
85	        sigma_schedule: Literal["exponential", "karras"] = "exponential",
86	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
87	        solver_order: int = 2,
88	        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "v_prediction",
89	        rho: float = 7.0,
90	        solver_type: Literal["midpoint", "heun"] = "midpoint",
91	        lower_order_final: bool = True,
92	        euler_at_final: bool = False,
93	        final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
94	    ) -> None:
95	        if solver_type not in ["midpoint", "heun"]:
96	            if solver_type in ["logrho", "bh1", "bh2"]:
97	                self.register_to_config(solver_type="midpoint")
98	            else:
99	                raise NotImplementedError(f"{solver_type} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")

self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775464158089-2kruff/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`

    - `test_steps_offset()`: Tests offset parameter

```

## Summary of Key Architectural Patterns:

1. **Inheritance Chain:** `DDIMScheduler` → `SchedulerMixin` + `ConfigMixin`
2. **Configuration Storage:** Via `@register_to_config` decorator → `self.config` (FrozenDict)

```

---

## Summary of Key Architectural Patterns:

1. **Inheritance Chain:** `DDIMScheduler` → `SchedulerMixin` + `ConfigMixin`
2. **Configuration Storage:** Via `@register_to_config` decorator → `self.config` (FrozenDict)
3. **Alpha/Beta Management:** Stored as instance attributes, computed in `__init__`
4. **Timestep Spacing:** Three modes (leading, trailing, linspace)
5. **Output Format:** Dataclass-based output objects (DDIMSchedulerOutput, SchedulerOutput)
6. **Testing Pattern:** Base test class with parametrized test methods and numerical validation

74	class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):

76	    Base class for all schedulers.

79	    functionalities.

82	    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.
83	
84	    Class attributes:
85	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
86	          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
87	          by parent class).
88	    """
89	
90	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
91	    _compatibles = []
92	    has_compatibles = True
93	
94	    @classmethod
95	    @validate_hf_hub_args

97	        cls,

99	        subfolder: str | None = None,
100	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ) -> Self:
103	        r"""
104	        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.
105	
106	        Parameters:
107	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
108	                Can be either:
109	
110	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
111	                      the Hub.
112	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
114	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
115	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
116	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
118	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
119	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
120	                is not used.
121	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
122	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
123	                cached versions if they exist.
124	
125	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
126	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
128	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
129	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
130	            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):

134	                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
135	                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
136	            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
137	                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
138	                allowed by Git.
139	
140	        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
141	        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >
142	        ["offline-mode"](https://huggingface.co/diffusers/installation.html#offline-mode) to use this method in a >
143	        firewalled environment.
144	
145	        """
146	        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
147	            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
148	            subfolder=subfolder,

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738	
739	
740	def flax_register_to_config(cls):
741	    original_init = cls.__init__
742	
743	    @functools.wraps(original_init)
744	    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
745	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
746	            raise RuntimeError(
747	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
748	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
749	            )
750	
751	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init. Retrieve all passed attributes
752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())
753	
754	        # Retrieve default values
755	        fields = dataclasses.fields(self)
756	        default_kwargs = {}
757	        for field in fields:
758	            # ignore flax specific attributes
759	            if field.name in self._flax_internal_args:
760	                continue
761	            if type(field.default) == dataclasses._MISSING_TYPE:
762	                default_kwargs[field.name] = None
763	            else:
764	                default_kwargs[field.name] = getattr(self, field.name)
765	
766	        # Make sure init_kwargs override default kwargs
767	        new_kwargs = {**default_kwargs, **init_kwargs}
768	        # dtype should be part of `init_kwargs`, but not `new_kwargs`
769	        if "dtype" in new_kwargs:
770	            new_kwargs.pop("dtype")
771	
772	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
773	        for i, arg in enumerate(args):
774	            name = fields[i].name
775	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
776	
777	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
778	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
779	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
780	
781	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
782	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
783	
784	    cls.__init__ = init
785	    return cls

24	class IPNDMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
25	    """

29	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.

33	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.

35	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
36	    """
37	
38	    order = 1
39	
40	    @register_to_config
41	    def __init__(
42	        self,
43	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
44	        trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
45	    ):
46	        # set `betas`, `alphas`, `timesteps`
47	        self.set_timesteps(num_train_timesteps)
48	
49	        # standard deviation of the initial noise distribution
50	        self.init_noise_sigma = 1.0
51	
52	        # For now we only support F-PNDM, i.e. the runge-kutta method
53	        # For more information on the algorithm please take a look at the paper: https://huggingface.co/papers/2202.09778
54	        # mainly at formula (9), (12), (13) and the Algorithm 2.
55	        self.pndm_order = 4
56	
57	        # running values
58	        self.ets = []
59	        self._step_index = None
60	        self._begin_index = None

66	
67	        Returns:
68	            `int` or `None`:
69	                The index counter for current timestep.
70	        """
71	        return self._step_index
72	
73	    @property

75	        """
76	        The index for the first timestep. It should be set from pipeline with `set_begin_index` method.
77

88

90	            begin_index (`int`, defaults to `0`):
91	                The begin index for the scheduler.

95	    def set_timesteps(self, num_inference_steps: int, device: str | torch.device | None = None):
96	        """
97	        Sets the discrete timesteps used for the diffusion chain (to be run before inference).
98	
99	        Args:
100	            num_inference_steps (`int`):
101	                The number of diffusion steps used when generating samples with a pre-trained model.
102	            device (`str` or `torch.device`, *optional*):
103	                The device to which the timesteps should be moved to. If `None`, the timesteps are not moved.
104	        """
105	        self.num_inference_steps = num_inference_steps
106	        steps = torch.linspace(1, 0, num_inference_steps + 1)[:-1]
107	        steps = torch.cat([steps, torch.tensor([0.0])])
108	
109	        if self.config.trained_betas is not None:
110	            self.betas = torch.tensor(self.config.trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
111	        else:
112	            self.betas = torch.sin(steps * math.pi / 2) ** 2
113	
114	        self.alphas = (1.0 - self.betas**2) ** 0.5
115	
116	        timesteps = (torch.atan2(self.betas, self.alphas) / math.pi * 2)[:-1]
117	        self.timesteps = timesteps.to(device)
118	
119	        self.ets = []
120	        self._step_index = None
121	        self._begin_index = None
122	
123	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler.index_for_timestep
124	    def index_for_timestep(
125	        self, timestep: float | torch.Tensor, schedule_timesteps: torch.Tensor | None = None
126	    ) -> int:
127	        """
128	        Find the index of a given timestep in the timestep schedule.
129	
130	        Args:
131	            timestep (`float` or `torch.Tensor`):
132	                The timestep value to find in the schedule.
133	            schedule_timesteps (`torch.Tensor`, *optional*):
134	                The timestep schedule to search in. If `None`, uses `self.timesteps`.
135	
136	        Returns:
137	            `int`:
138	                The index of the timestep in the schedule. For the very first step, returns the second index if
139	                multiple matches exist to avoid skipping a sigma when starting mid-schedule (e.g., for image-to-image).
140	        """
141	        if schedule_timesteps is None:
142	            schedule_timesteps = self.timesteps
143	
144	        indices = (schedule_timesteps == timestep).nonzero()
145	
146	        # The sigma index that is taken for the **very** first `step`
147	        # is always the second index (or the last index if there is only 1)
148	        # This way we can ensure we don't accidentally skip a sigma in
149	        # case we start in the middle of the denoising schedule (e.g. for image-to-image)
150	        pos = 1 if len(indices) > 1 else 0
151	
152	        return indices[pos].item()

13	# CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler
14	
15	The [`CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler`] is a variant of [`DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`] with cosine schedule, proposed by Nichol and Dhariwal (2021).
16	It is being used in the [Stable Audio Open](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.14358) paper and the [Stability-AI/stable-audio-tool](https://github.com/Stability-AI/stable-audio-tools) codebase.
17	
18	This scheduler was contributed by [Yoach Lacombe](https://huggingface.co/ylacombe).

13	# Schedulers
14	
15	🤗 Diffusers provides many scheduler functions for the diffusion process. A scheduler takes a model's output (the sample which the diffusion process is iterating on) and a timestep to return a denoised sample. The timestep is important because it dictates where in the diffusion process the step is; data is generated by iterating forward *n* timesteps and inference occurs by propagating backward through the timesteps. Based on the timestep, a scheduler may be *discrete* in which case the timestep is an `int` or *continuous* in which case the timestep is a `float`.
16	
17	Depending on the context, a scheduler defines how to iteratively add noise to an image or how to update a sample based on a model's output:
18	
19	- during *training*, a scheduler adds noise (there are different algorithms for how to add noise) to a sample to train a diffusion model
20	- during *inference*, a scheduler defines how to update a sample based on a pretrained model's output
21	
22	Many schedulers are implemented from the [k-diffusion](https://github.com/crowsonkb/k-diffusion) library by [Katherine Crowson](https://github.com/crowsonkb/), and they're also widely used in A1111. To help you map the schedulers from k-diffusion and A1111 to the schedulers in 🤗 Diffusers, take a look at the table below:
23	
24	| A1111/k-diffusion    | 🤗 Diffusers                         | Usage                                                                                                         |
25	|---------------------|-------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
26	| DPM++ 2M            | [`DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`]     |                                                                                                               |
27	| DPM++ 2M Karras     | [`DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`]     | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                                                            |
28	| DPM++ 2M SDE        | [`DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`]     | init with `algorithm_type="sde-dpmsolver++"`                                                                  |
29	| DPM++ 2M SDE Karras | [`DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`]     | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True` and `algorithm_type="sde-dpmsolver++"`                                     |
30	| DPM++ 2S a          | N/A                                 | very similar to  `DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler`                         |
31	| DPM++ 2S a Karras   | N/A                                 | very similar to  `DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler(use_karras_sigmas=True, ...)` |
32	| DPM++ SDE           | [`DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler`]    |                                                                                                               |
33	| DPM++ SDE Karras    | [`DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler`]    | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                                                            |
34	| DPM2                | [`KDPM2DiscreteScheduler`]          |                                                                                                               |
35	| DPM2 Karras         | [`KDPM2DiscreteScheduler`]          | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                                                            |
36	| DPM2 a              | [`KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler`] |                                                                                                               |
37	| DPM2 a Karras       | [`KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler`] | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                                                            |
38	| DPM adaptive        | N/A                                 |                                                                                                               |
39	| DPM fast            | N/A                                 |                                                                                                               |
40	| Euler               | [`EulerDiscreteScheduler`]          |                                                                                                               |
41	| Euler a             | [`EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler`] |                                                                                                               |
42	| Heun                | [`HeunDiscreteScheduler`]           |                                                                                                               |
43	| LMS                 | [`LMSDiscreteScheduler`]            |                                                                                                               |
44	| LMS Karras          | [`LMSDiscreteScheduler`]            | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                                                            |
45	| N/A                 | [`DEISMultistepScheduler`]          |                                                                                                               |
46	| N/A                 | [`UniPCMultistepScheduler`]         |                                                                                                               |
47	
48	## Noise schedules and schedule types
49	| A1111/k-diffusion        | 🤗 Diffusers                                                               |
50	|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
51	| Karras                   | init with `use_karras_sigmas=True`                                         |
52	| sgm_uniform              | init with `timestep_spacing="trailing"`                                    |
53	| simple                   | init with `timestep_spacing="trailing"`                                    |
54	| exponential              | init with `timestep_spacing="linspace"`, `use_exponential_sigmas=True`     |
55	| beta                     | init with `timestep_spacing="linspace"`, `use_beta_sigmas=True`            |
56	
57	All schedulers are built from the base [`SchedulerMixin`] class which implements low level utilities shared by all schedulers.
58

65	## KarrasDiffusionSchedulers
66	
67	[`KarrasDiffusionSchedulers`] are a broad generalization of schedulers in 🤗 Diffusers. The schedulers in this class are distinguished at a high level by their noise sampling strategy, the type of network and scaling, the training strategy, and how the loss is weighed.
68	
69	The different schedulers in this class, depending on the ordinary differential equations (ODE) solver type, fall into the above taxonomy and provide a good abstraction for the design of the main schedulers implemented in 🤗 Diffusers. The schedulers in this class are given [here](https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/blob/a69754bb879ed55b9b6dc9dd0b3cf4fa4124c765/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py#L32).
70

50	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.betas_for_alpha_bar
51	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
52	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
53	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
54	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
55	) -> torch.Tensor:
56	    """
57	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
58	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
59	
60	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
61	    to that part of the diffusion process.
62	
63	    Args:
64	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
65	            The number of betas to produce.
66	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
67	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
68	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
69	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
70	
71	    Returns:
72	        `torch.Tensor`:
73	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
74	    """
75	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
76	
77	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
78	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
79	
80	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
81	
82	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
83	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
84	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
85	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
86	
87	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
88	
89	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
90	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
91	
92	    else:
93	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
94	
95	    betas = []
96	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
97	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
98	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
99	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
100	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)

103	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
104	    """
105	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
106	
107	    Args:
108	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
109	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
110	
111	    Returns:
112	        `torch.Tensor`:
113	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
114	    """
115	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
116	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
117	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
118	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
119	
120	    # Store old values.
121	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
122	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
123	
124	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
125	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
126	
127	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
128	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
129	
130	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
131	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
132	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
133	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
134	    betas = 1 - alphas
135	
136	    return betas

145	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.

157	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Summary: Hugging Face Diffusers Schedulers Module Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the diffusers library codebase, here's a complete understanding of the schedulers module to help you create a new CosineAnnealingScheduler:

---

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/`

Contains 50+ scheduler files with this naming pattern: `scheduling_<scheduler_type>.py`

Key files:
- `__init__.py` - Central registry and exports
- `scheduling_utils.py` - Base classes and utilities
- `scheduling_ddpm.py` - Foundational DDPM scheduler
- `scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM variant
- `deprecated/` - Legacy schedulers
- Test files in `/tests/schedulers/`

---

### 2. BASE CLASS ARCHITECTURE

**SchedulerMixin** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 74-190):
```python
class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME  # "scheduler_config.json"
    _compatibles = []  # List of compatible scheduler classes
    has_compatibles = True
    
    # Key methods:
    @classmethod
    def from_pretrained(cls, pretrained_model_name_or_path, ...)
    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory, ...)
    @property
    def compatibles(self) -> list[SchedulerMixin]
```

**SchedulerOutput** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 60-71):
```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
```

**Pattern:** All schedulers inherit from BOTH `SchedulerMixin` and `ConfigMixin`:
```python
class YourScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
```

---

### 3. CONFIGURATION & REGISTRATION PATTERN

**@register_to_config decorator** (`configuration_utils.py` lines 690-737):

This decorator **automatically saves all `__init__` parameters** to the scheduler's configuration. It:
- Captures all init parameters
- Stores them in `self._internal_dict` (a FrozenDict)
- Makes config accessible via `scheduler.config.parameter_name`
- Respects `ignore_for_config` class variable for parameters to exclude
- Filters out private kwargs (starting with `_`)

**Example usage in DDPMScheduler:**
```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    @register_to_config
    def __init__(
        self,
        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
        beta_end: float = 0.02,
        beta_schedule: Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2", "sigmoid"] = "linear",
        # ... more parameters
    ):
        # Implementation
```

**ConfigMixin** provides:
- `save_config(save_directory)` - Saves config to JSON
- `from_config(config_dict, **kwargs)` - Loads from config
- `from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path)` - Downloads & loads
- Auto-storage of init parameters via `@register_to_config`

---

### 4. DDPM SCHEDULER STRUCTURE (Template to Follow)

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (669 lines)

**Key Components:**

1. **Helper Functions** (before class):
   - `betas_for_alpha_bar()` - Creates beta schedule
   - `rescale_zero_terminal_snr()` - Rescales betas for numerical stability

2. **Output Dataclass**:
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
       prev_sample: torch.Tensor
       pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
   ```

3. **Class Declaration**:
   ```python
   class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
       _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
       order = 1  # Numerical order (1=first-order method)
   ```

4. **Init Method** (with @register_to_config):
   - Defines all config parameters
   - Computes derived values (`self.betas`, `self.alphas`, `self.alphas_cumprod`)
   - Initializes state: `self.num_inference_steps`, `self.timesteps`, `self.init_noise_sigma`

5. **Core Methods**:
   - `scale_model_input(sample, timestep)` - Optional input scaling
   - `set_timesteps(num_inference_steps, device, timesteps)` - Sets up inference schedule
   - `step(model_output, timestep, sample, generator)` - Main denoising step
   - `add_noise(original_samples, noise, timesteps)` - Forward diffusion
   - `get_velocity(sample, noise, timesteps)` - Velocity computation
   - `__len__()` - Returns `num_train_timesteps`
   - `previous_timestep(timestep)` - Gets previous timestep in schedule

6. **Helper Methods**:
   - `_get_variance(t, predicted_variance, variance_type)` - Variance computation
   - `_threshold_sample(sample)` - Dynamic thresholding for stability

---

### 5. DDIM SCHEDULER KEY DIFFERENCES

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` (500+ lines)

DDIM differs from DDPM:
- **Deterministic denoising** (fewer steps needed)
- `set_alpha_to_one` parameter for final alpha handling
- `_get_variance()` uses different formula
- `set_timesteps()` supports custom timestep spacing: "leading", "trailing", "linspace"
- Same base inheritance pattern

**Key Pattern Difference:**
```python
# DDPM: steps through all timesteps
# DDIM: Can skip timesteps via set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=50)
scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=50)
for t in scheduler.timesteps:
    output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample)
```

---

### 6. SCHEDULER EXPORTS & REGISTRATION

**Level 1:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py` (231 lines)

Uses lazy loading pattern with `_LazyModule`:

```python
_import_structure = {}

# Conditional imports based on dependencies
try:
    if not is_torch_available():
        raise OptionalDependencyNotAvailable()
except OptionalDependencyNotAvailable:
    from ..utils import dummy_pt_objects
    _dummy_modules.update(get_objects_from_module(dummy_pt_objects))
else:
    _import_structure["scheduling_ddpm"] = ["DDPMScheduler"]
    _import_structure["scheduling_ddim"] = ["DDIMScheduler"]
    # ... more schedulers

# TYPE_CHECKING block for actual imports when needed
if TYPE_CHECKING or DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT:
    from .scheduling_ddpm import DDPMScheduler
    from .scheduling_ddim import DDIMScheduler
else:
    # Use lazy loading
    sys.modules[__name__] = _LazyModule(__name__, globals()["__file__"], _import_structure, module_spec=__spec__)
```

**Level 2:** `/src/diffusers/__init__.py` (1500+ lines)

Re-exports all schedulers to top-level diffusers namespace:

```python
from diffusers.schedulers import (
    DDPMScheduler,
    DDIMScheduler,
    # ... all other schedulers
)

__all__ = [
    "DDPMScheduler",
    "DDIMScheduler",
    # ... listed in _import_structure
]
```

---

### 7. TEST PATTERNS

**Base Test Class:** `/tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py` (400+ lines)

**SchedulerCommonTest** provides:
- `dummy_sample` - 4x3x8x8 batch
- `dummy_noise_deter` - Deterministic noise
- `dummy_sample_deter` - Deterministic sample
- `dummy_model()` - Dummy denoising model
- `get_scheduler_config()` - Must be overridden by subclass
- `check_over_configs(**config)` - Tests scheduler with config variations
- `check_over_forward(time_step=...)` - Tests forward pass

**Specific Test Example** (`test_scheduler_ddpm.py`):

```python
class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DDPMScheduler,)

    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
            "clip_sample": True,
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config

    def test_timesteps(self):
        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)

    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
        scheduler = self.scheduler_classes[0](**self.get_scheduler_config())
        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
        model = self.dummy_model()
        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)

        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
            residual = model(sample, t)
            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
            sample = pred_prev_sample

        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
```

---

### 8. REGISTRATION TO DIFFUSERS ENUM

**KarrasDiffusionSchedulers** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 33-49):

```python
class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    DDIMScheduler = 1
    DDPMScheduler = 2
    PNDMScheduler = 3
    EulerDiscreteScheduler = 5
    # ... etc
```

Used for `_compatibles`:
```python
_compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
```

---

### 9. CONFIGURATION FILE FORMAT

When saved via `save_pretrained()`, generates `scheduler_config.json`:

```json
{
  "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
  "beta_start": 0.0001,
  "beta_end": 0.02,
  "beta_schedule": "linear",
  "variance_type": "fixed_small",
  "clip_sample": true,
  "clip_sample_range": 1.0,
  "prediction_type": "epsilon",
  "thresholding": false,
  "dynamic_thresholding_ratio": 0.995,
  "sample_max_value": 1.0,
  "timestep_spacing": "leading",
  "steps_offset": 0,
  "rescale_betas_zero_snr": false,
  "_class_name": "DDPMScheduler",
  "_diffusers_version": "0.28.0"
}
```

---

### 10. KEY PATTERNS TO REPLICATE FOR CosineAnnealingScheduler

1. **File naming:** `scheduling_cosine_annealing.py`

2. **Imports required:**
   ```python
   from dataclasses import dataclass
   from typing import Literal
   import numpy as np
   import torch
   from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
   from ..utils import BaseOutput
   from ..utils.torch_utils import randn_tensor
   from .scheduling_utils import SchedulerMixin
   ```

3. **Structure:**
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class CosineAnnealingSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
       prev_sample: torch.Tensor
       pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None

   class CosineAnnealingScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
       _compatibles = []  # or add compatible schedulers
       order = 1

       @register_to_config
       def __init__(self, num_train_timesteps=1000, ...):
           # Initialize config params
           # Compute derived values
           pass

       def set_timesteps(self, num_inference_steps, device=None, timesteps=None):
           # Create timestep schedule
           pass

       def step(self, model_output, timestep, sample, generator=None, return_dict=True):
           # Main denoising step
           pass

       def __len__(self):
           return self.config.num_train_timesteps
   ```

4. **Registration in `__init__.py`:**
   ```python
   _import_structure["scheduling_cosine_annealing"] = ["CosineAnnealingScheduler"]
   
   # In TYPE_CHECKING block:
   from .scheduling_cosine_annealing import CosineAnnealingScheduler
   ```

5. **Add to top-level `src/diffusers/__init__.py`**

6. **Create test file** `tests/schedulers/test_scheduler_cosine_annealing.py`

7. **Optional: Add to KarrasDiffusionSchedulers enum** if compatible with other schedulers

---

### 11. IMPORTANT IMPLEMENTATION NOTES

- **`init_noise_sigma = 1.0`** - Standard for most schedulers
- **`self.timesteps`** - Must be a torch.Tensor on the correct device
- **`self.alphas_cumprod`** - Cumulative product of alphas, drives noise level
- **`scale_model_input()`** - Some schedulers need to scale inputs (most return sample unchanged)
- **`add_noise()`** - For training/inference with noise addition
- **`_step_index` tracking** - Optional but useful for multi-step schedulers
- **Device handling** - Move tensors to correct device in `set_timesteps()`
- **Type hints** - Use `Literal` for constrained string parameters

---

### 12. TESTING CHECKLIST

- Override `get_scheduler_config()` with valid defaults
- Test config variations (timesteps, beta ranges, schedules)
- Test prediction types (epsilon, sample, v_prediction) if supported
- Test clipping/thresholding if implemented
- Test custom timesteps via `set_timesteps(timesteps=...)`
- Test full denoising loops (forward pass through all steps)
- Test deterministic outputs with fixed seeds
- Test device movement (CPU/GPU)
- Test config save/load via `save_config()` and `from_config()`

---

This architecture enables diffusers to:
- ✅ Support 50+ different scheduler variants
- ✅ Load/save configs from HuggingFace Hub
- ✅ Swap schedulers in pipelines seamlessly
- ✅ Maintain backward compatibility
- ✅ Support lazy loading for fast imports
- ✅ Handle optional dependencies gracefully

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Summary: Hugging Face Diffusers Schedulers Module Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the diffusers library codebase, here's a complete understanding of the schedulers module to help you create a new CosineAnnealingScheduler:

---

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/`

Contains 50+ scheduler files with this naming pattern: `scheduling_<scheduler_type>.py`

Key files:
- `__init__.py` - Central registry and exports
- `scheduling_utils.py` - Base classes and utilities
- `scheduling_ddpm.py` - Foundational DDPM scheduler
- `scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM variant
- `deprecated/` - Legacy schedulers
- Test files in `/tests/schedulers/`

---

### 2. BASE CLASS ARCHITECTURE

**SchedulerMixin** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 74-190):
```python
class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME  # "scheduler_config.json"
    _compatibles = []  # List of compatible scheduler classes
    has_compatibles = True
    
    # Key methods:
    @classmethod
    def from_pretrained(cls, pretrained_model_name_or_path, ...)
    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory, ...)
    @property
    def compatibles(self) -> list[SchedulerMixin]
```

**SchedulerOutput** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 60-71):
```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
```

**Pattern:** All schedulers inherit from BOTH `SchedulerMixin` and `ConfigMixin`:
```python
class YourScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
```

---

### 3. CONFIGURATION & REGISTRATION PATTERN

**@register_to_config decorator** (`configuration_utils.py` lines 690-737):

This decorator **automatically saves all `__init__` parameters** to the scheduler's configuration. It:
- Captures all init parameters
- Stores them in `self._internal_dict` (a FrozenDict)
- Makes config accessible via `scheduler.config.parameter_name`
- Respects `ignore_for_config` class variable for parameters to exclude
- Filters out private kwargs (starting with `_`)

**Example usage in DDPMScheduler:**
```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    @register_to_config
    def __init__(
        self,
        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
        beta_end: float = 0.02,
        beta_schedule: Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2", "sigmoid"] = "linear",
        # ... more parameters
    ):
        # Implementation
```

**ConfigMixin** provides:
- `save_config(save_directory)` - Saves config to JSON
- `from_config(config_dict, **kwargs)` - Loads from config
- `from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path)` - Downloads & loads
- Auto-storage of init parameters via `@register_to_config`

---

### 4. DDPM SCHEDULER STRUCTURE (Template to Follow)

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (669 lines)

**Key Components:**

1. **Helper Functions** (before class):
   - `betas_for_alpha_bar()` - Creates beta schedule
   - `rescale_zero_terminal_snr()` - Rescales betas for numerical stability

2. **Output Dataclass**:
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
       prev_sample: torch.Tensor
       pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
   ```

3. **Class Declaration**:
   ```python
   class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
       _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
       order = 1  # Numerical order (1=first-order method)
   ```

4. **Init Method** (with @register_to_config):
   - Defines all config parameters
   - Computes derived values (`self.betas`, `self.alphas`, `self.alphas_cumprod`)
   - Initializes state: `self.num_inference_steps`, `self.timesteps`, `self.init_noise_sigma`

5. **Core Methods**:
   - `scale_model_input(sample, timestep)` - Optional input scaling
   - `set_timesteps(num_inference_steps, device, timesteps)` - Sets up inference schedule
   - `step(model_output, timestep, sample, generator)` - Main denoising step
   - `add_noise(original_samples, noise, timesteps)` - Forward diffusion
   - `get_velocity(sample, noise, timesteps)` - Velocity computation
   - `__len__()` - Returns `num_train_timesteps`
   - `previous_timestep(timestep)` - Gets previous timestep in schedule

6. **Helper Methods**:
   - `_get_variance(t, predicted_variance, variance_type)` - Variance computation
   - `_threshold_sample(sample)` - Dynamic thresholding for stability

---

### 5. DDIM SCHEDULER KEY DIFFERENCES

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` (500+ lines)

DDIM differs from DDPM:
- **Deterministic denoising** (fewer steps needed)
- `set_alpha_to_one` parameter for final alpha handling
- `_get_variance()` uses different formula
- `set_timesteps()` supports custom timestep spacing: "leading", "trailing", "linspace"
- Same base inheritance pattern

**Key Pattern Difference:**
```python
# DDPM: steps through all timesteps
# DDIM: Can skip timesteps via set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=50)
scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=50)
for t in scheduler.timesteps:
    output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample)
```

---

### 6. SCHEDULER EXPORTS & REGISTRATION

**Level 1:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py` (231 lines)

Uses lazy loading pattern with `_LazyModule`:

```python
_import_structure = {}

# Conditional imports based on dependencies
try:
    if not is_torch_available():
        raise OptionalDependencyNotAvailable()
except OptionalDependencyNotAvailable:
    from ..utils import dummy_pt_objects
    _dummy_modules.update(get_objects_from_module(dummy_pt_objects))
else:
    _import_structure["scheduling_ddpm"] = ["DDPMScheduler"]
    _import_structure["scheduling_ddim"] = ["DDIMScheduler"]
    # ... more schedulers

# TYPE_CHECKING block for actual imports when needed
if TYPE_CHECKING or DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT:
    from .scheduling_ddpm import DDPMScheduler
    from .scheduling_ddim import DDIMScheduler
else:
    # Use lazy loading
    sys.modules[__name__] = _LazyModule(__name__, globals()["__file__"], _import_structure, module_spec=__spec__)
```

**Level 2:** `/src/diffusers/__init__.py` (1500+ lines)

Re-exports all schedulers to top-level diffusers namespace:

```python
from diffusers.schedulers import (
    DDPMScheduler,
    DDIMScheduler,
    # ... all other schedulers
)

__all__ = [
    "DDPMScheduler",
    "DDIMScheduler",
    # ... listed in _import_structure
]
```

---

### 7. TEST PATTERNS

**Base Test Class:** `/tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py` (400+ lines)

**SchedulerCommonTest** provides:
- `dummy_sample` - 4x3x8x8 batch
- `dummy_noise_deter` - Deterministic noise
- `dummy_sample_deter` - Deterministic sample
- `dummy_model()` - Dummy denoising model
- `get_scheduler_config()` - Must be overridden by subclass
- `check_over_configs(**config)` - Tests scheduler with config variations
- `check_over_forward(time_step=...)` - Tests forward pass

**Specific Test Example** (`test_scheduler_ddpm.py`):

```python
class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DDPMScheduler,)

    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
            "clip_sample": True,
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config

    def test_timesteps(self):
        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)

    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
        scheduler = self.scheduler_classes[0](**self.get_scheduler_config())
        num_trained_timesteps = len(scheduler)
        model = self.dummy_model()
        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)

        for t in reversed(range(num_trained_timesteps)):
            residual = model(sample, t)
            pred_prev_sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, generator=generator).prev_sample
            sample = pred_prev_sample

        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 258.9606) < 1e-2
```

---

### 8. REGISTRATION TO DIFFUSERS ENUM

**KarrasDiffusionSchedulers** (`scheduling_utils.py` lines 33-49):

```python
class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    DDIMScheduler = 1
    DDPMScheduler = 2
    PNDMScheduler = 3
    EulerDiscreteScheduler = 5
    # ... etc
```

Used for `_compatibles`:
```python
_compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
```

---

### 9. CONFIGURATION FILE FORMAT

When saved via `save_pretrained()`, generates `scheduler_config.json`:

```json
{
  "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
  "beta_start": 0.0001,
  "beta_end": 0.02,
  "beta_schedule": "linear",
  "variance_type": "fixed_small",
  "clip_sample": true,
  "clip_sample_range": 1.0,
  "prediction_type": "epsilon",
  "thresholding": false,
  "dynamic_thresholding_ratio": 0.995,
  "sample_max_value": 1.0,
  "timestep_spacing": "leading",
  "steps_offset": 0,
  "rescale_betas_zero_snr": false,
  "_class_name": "DDPMScheduler",
  "_diffusers_version": "0.28.0"
}
```

---

### 10. KEY PATTERNS TO REPLICATE FOR CosineAnnealingScheduler

1. **File naming:** `scheduling_cosine_annealing.py`

2. **Imports required:**
   ```python

   from typing import Literal
   import numpy as np
   import torch
   from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
   from ..utils import BaseOutput
   from ..utils.torch_utils import randn_tensor
   from .scheduling_utils import SchedulerMixin
   ```

3. **Structure:**
   ```python

   class CosineAnnealingSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
       prev_sample: torch.Tensor
       pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None

   class CosineAnnealingScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
       _compatibles = []  # or add compatible schedulers
       order = 1

       @register_to_config
       def __init__(self, num_train_timesteps=1000, ...):
           # Initialize config params

           pass

           # Create timestep schedule
           pass

           # Main denoising step
           pass

       def __len__(self):
           return self.config.num_train_timesteps
   ```

4. **Registration in `__init__.py`:**
   ```python

   ```

5. **Add to top-level `src/diffusers/__init__.py`**

6. **Create test file** `tests/schedulers/test_scheduler_cosine_annealing.py`

7. **Optional: Add to KarrasDiffusionSchedulers enum** if compatible with other schedulers

---

### 11. IMPORTANT IMPLEMENTATION NOTES

Perfect! Now let me compile the complete exploration report.

## Comprehensive Scheduler Registration Mechanism Exploration

I've thoroughly explored the Hugging Face diffusers library codebase to understand the complete scheduler registration mechanism. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Scheduler Registration Across __init__.py Files**

**Key Files:**
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py` - Scheduler module initialization
- `/src/diffusers/__init__.py` - Top-level library exports

**Registration Pattern in `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py`:**

The file uses a **lazy-loading pattern** with conditional imports based on optional dependencies:

```python
_import_structure = {}
_dummy_modules = {}

# Base torch-only schedulers (lines 41-81)
_import_structure["scheduling_amused"] = ["AmusedScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_consistency_decoder"] = ["ConsistencyDecoderScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_ddim"] = ["DDIMScheduler"]
# ... 30+ more schedulers

# Flax-specific schedulers (lines 92-105)
_import_structure["scheduling_ddim_flax"] = ["FlaxDDIMScheduler"]
# ... more Flax variants

# Scipy+torch dependent (lines 117)
_import_structure["scheduling_lms_discrete"] = ["LMSDiscreteScheduler"]

# Torchsde+torch dependent (lines 128-129)
_import_structure["scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep"] = ["CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_dpmsolver_sde"] = ["DPMSolverSDEScheduler"]
```

**Conditional Registration Structure:**
- Lines 32-38: Torch availability check (fallback to dummy objects)
- Lines 84-105: Flax availability check
- Lines 109-117: Torch + Scipy check
- Lines 120-129: Torch + Torchsde check

**Top-level Registration in `/src/diffusers/__init__.py`:**

The schedulers are aggregated at the top level:
- Lines 46: Initialize empty schedulers list: `"schedulers": []`
- Lines 341-384: Extend with base torch schedulers via `_import_structure["schedulers"].extend([...])`
- Line 398: Extend with scipy-dependent schedulers
- Line 411: Extend with torchsde-dependent schedulers (including `CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler`)
- Lines 1112-1170: Conditional imports in TYPE_CHECKING/DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT block

### 2. **Auto-Mapping and Auto-Registration Files**

**Dummy Object Files (for optional dependencies):**

Located in `/src/diffusers/utils/`:
- `dummy_pt_objects.py` - Placeholder classes when PyTorch not available (contains 36+ scheduler dummy classes, lines 2461-3061)
- `dummy_torch_and_scipy_objects.py` - Dummy `LMSDiscreteScheduler` (requires torch + scipy)
- `dummy_torch_and_torchsde_objects.py` - Dummy `CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler` and `DPMSolverSDEScheduler` (requires torch + torchsde)
- `dummy_flax_objects.py` - Dummy Flax scheduler classes

**Dummy Structure Pattern:**
```python
class CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler(metaclass=DummyObject):
    _backends = ["torch", "torchsde"]
    
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        requires_backends(self, ["torch", "torchsde"])
```

### 3. **Scheduler Base Classes and Mixins**

**Base Classes (`/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`):**
- `SchedulerMixin` - Base class providing `from_pretrained()`, `save_pretrained()`, and compatibility checking
- `KarrasDiffusionSchedulers` - Enum listing compatible scheduler classes (lines 33-49)
- `SchedulerOutput` - Dataclass for step output
- `AysSchedules` - Dictionary of pre-defined schedules

## Comprehensive Scheduler Registration Mechanism Exploration

I've thoroughly explored the Hugging Face diffusers library codebase to understand the complete scheduler registration mechanism. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Scheduler Registration Across __init__.py Files**

**Registration Pattern in `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py`:**

The file uses a **lazy-loading pattern** with conditional imports based on optional dependencies:

```python
_import_structure = {}
_dummy_modules = {}

# Base torch-only schedulers (lines 41-81)
_import_structure["scheduling_amused"] = ["AmusedScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_consistency_decoder"] = ["ConsistencyDecoderScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_ddim"] = ["DDIMScheduler"]

# Flax-specific schedulers (lines 92-105)

# ... more Flax variants

# Scipy+torch dependent (lines 117)
_import_structure["scheduling_lms_discrete"] = ["LMSDiscreteScheduler"]

# Torchsde+torch dependent (lines 128-129)
_import_structure["scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep"] = ["CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler"]
_import_structure["scheduling_dpmsolver_sde"] = ["DPMSolverSDEScheduler"]
```

**Conditional Registration Structure:**
- Lines 32-38: Torch availability check (fallback to dummy objects)
- Lines 84-105: Flax availability check
- Lines 109-117: Torch + Scipy check
- Lines 120-129: Torch + Torchsde check

**Top-level Registration in `/src/diffusers/__init__.py`:**

The schedulers are aggregated at the top level:
- Lines 46: Initialize empty schedulers list: `"schedulers": []`
- Lines 341-384: Extend with base torch schedulers via `_import_structure["schedulers"].extend([...])`
- Line 398: Extend with scipy-dependent schedulers
- Line 411: Extend with torchsde-dependent schedulers (including `CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler`)
- Lines 1112-1170: Conditional imports in TYPE_CHECKING/DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT block

### 2. **Auto-Mapping and Auto-Registration Files**

- `dummy_torch_and_torchsde_objects.py` - Dummy `CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler` and `DPMSolverSDEScheduler` (requires torch + torchsde)

14	import importlib
15	import os
16	from dataclasses import dataclass
17	from enum import Enum
18	
19	import torch
20	from huggingface_hub.utils import validate_hf_hub_args
21	from typing_extensions import Self
22	
23	from ..utils import BaseOutput, PushToHubMixin
24	
25	
26	SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME = "scheduler_config.json"
27	
28	
29	# NOTE: We make this type an enum because it simplifies usage in docs and prevents
30	# circular imports when used for `_compatibles` within the schedulers module.
31	# When it's used as a type in pipelines, it really is a Union because the actual
32	# scheduler instance is passed in.
33	class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
34	    DDIMScheduler = 1
35	    DDPMScheduler = 2
36	    PNDMScheduler = 3
37	    LMSDiscreteScheduler = 4
38	    EulerDiscreteScheduler = 5
39	    HeunDiscreteScheduler = 6
40	    EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler = 7
41	    DPMSolverMultistepScheduler = 8
42	    DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler = 9
43	    KDPM2DiscreteScheduler = 10
44	    KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler = 11
45	    DEISMultistepScheduler = 12
46	    UniPCMultistepScheduler = 13
47	    DPMSolverSDEScheduler = 14
48	    EDMEulerScheduler = 15
49	
50	
51	AysSchedules = {
52	    "StableDiffusionTimesteps": [999, 850, 736, 645, 545, 455, 343, 233, 124, 24],
53	    "StableDiffusionSigmas": [14.615, 6.475, 3.861, 2.697, 1.886, 1.396, 0.963, 0.652, 0.399, 0.152, 0.0],
54	    "StableDiffusionXLTimesteps": [999, 845, 730, 587, 443, 310, 193, 116, 53, 13],
55	    "StableDiffusionXLSigmas": [14.615, 6.315, 3.771, 2.181, 1.342, 0.862, 0.555, 0.380, 0.234, 0.113, 0.0],
56	    "StableDiffusionVideoSigmas": [700.00, 54.5, 15.886, 7.977, 4.248, 1.789, 0.981, 0.403, 0.173, 0.034, 0.0],
57	}
58	
59	
60	@dataclass
61	class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
62	    """
63	    Base class for the output of a scheduler's `step` function.
64	
65	    Args:
66	        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
67	            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
68	            denoising loop.
69	    """
70	
71	    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
72	
73	
74	class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
75	    """
76	    Base class for all schedulers.
77	
78	    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
79	    functionalities.
80	
81	    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
82	    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.
83	
84	    Class attributes:
85	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
86	          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
87	          by parent class).
88	    """
89	
90	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
91	    _compatibles = []
92	    has_compatibles = True
93	
94	    @classmethod
95	    @validate_hf_hub_args
96	    def from_pretrained(
97	        cls,
98	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
99	        subfolder: str | None = None,
100	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ) -> Self:
103	        r"""
104	        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.
105	
106	        Parameters:
107	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
108	                Can be either:
109	
110	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
111	                      the Hub.
112	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
114	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
115	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
116	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
118	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
119	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
120	                is not used.
121	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
122	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
123	                cached versions if they exist.
124	
125	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
126	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
128	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
129	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
130	            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
131	                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
132	                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
133	            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
134	                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
135	                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
136	            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
137	                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
138	                allowed by Git.
139	
140	        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
141	        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >
142	        ["offline-mode"](https://huggingface.co/diffusers/installation.html#offline-mode) to use this method in a >
143	        firewalled environment.
144	
145	        """
146	        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
147	            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
148	            subfolder=subfolder,
149	            return_unused_kwargs=True,
150	            return_commit_hash=True,
151	            **kwargs,
152	        )
153	        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)
154	
155	    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
156	        """
157	        Save a scheduler configuration object to a directory so that it can be reloaded using the
158	        [`~SchedulerMixin.from_pretrained`] class method.
159	
160	        Args:
161	            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
162	                Directory where the configuration JSON file will be saved (will be created if it does not exist).
163	            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
164	                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
165	                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
166	                namespace).
167	            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
168	                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
169	        """
170	        self.save_config(save_directory=save_directory, push_to_hub=push_to_hub, **kwargs)
171	
172	    @property
173	    def compatibles(self):
174	        """
175	        Returns all schedulers that are compatible with this scheduler
176	
177	        Returns:
178	            `list[SchedulerMixin]`: list of compatible schedulers
179	        """
180	        return self._get_compatibles()
181	
182	    @classmethod
183	    def _get_compatibles(cls):
184	        compatible_classes_str = list(set([cls.__name__] + cls._compatibles))
185	        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
186	        compatible_classes = [
187	            getattr(diffusers_library, c) for c in compatible_classes_str if hasattr(diffusers_library, c)
188	        ]
189	        return compatible_classes
